description_long: This helicopter features a robust, angular fuselage with a predominantly gray and blue color scheme, accented by military-style markings and a shark mouth graphic on the nose. Two large, cylindrical engines or weapon pods are mounted above the main body, extending forward and giving the craft a distinctive, aggressive silhouette. The cockpit is enclosed with a rounded, multi-panel window, providing wide visibility for the pilot, and the side doors appear reinforced. The tail section is compact, with a vertical stabilizer and no visible tail rotor, suggesting a possible alternative stabilization system. The landing gear consists of sturdy, angular skids designed for rough terrain. The overall design suggests a heavily armored, combat-oriented helicopter suitable for urban or battlefield environments.
description: A heavily armored, gray and blue combat helicopter with dual forward-mounted pods, a shark mouth nose graphic, and reinforced cockpit windows. It features angular landing skids and military markings.
description_view_0: Top-down view showing the dual forward-mounted pods, angular wings, and the full extent of the shark mouth graphic on the nose.
description_view_1: Rear oblique view highlighting the compact tail section, the underside of the pods, and the angular landing skids.
description_view_2: Side view displaying the full profile of the fuselage, cockpit window arrangement, and military insignia on the tail and wings.
description_view_3: Bottom oblique view revealing the underside details, landing skids, and the arrangement of the cockpit and side doors.
attribute_rotor_blades: Two long, narrow main rotor blades are visible, appearing to be made of composite material with a gray and blue color scheme and minimal markings.
attribute_tail_rotor: Not visible; the tail section does not show a conventional tail rotor, possibly indicating a NOTAR or alternative stabilization system.
attribute_landing_skids: Angular, robust landing skids made of metal, painted gray with black footpads and reinforced crossbars for stability.
attribute_cockpit_windows: The cockpit features a multi-panel, rounded window arrangement with clear glass, black framing, and no visible tint or markings.
